wiki.summary: "Damn" is a song by American rapper and producer Ye (formerly Kanye West) from his twelfth studio album, Bully (2026). It was released as a streaming single on June 20, 2025, as part of the first Bully EP, released alongside "Preacher Man" and "Beauty and the Beast." Produced by Ye with co-writing credits from his former manager and collaborator Hassan Khaffaf, the track is a concise, two-minute reflection on fame and consequence. The production of "Damn" is built around a melancholic piano melody and a raw, unpolished drum break. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: "Damn" is a song by American rapper and producer Ye (formerly Kanye West) from his twelfth studio album, Bully (2026). It was released as a streaming single on June 20, 2025, as part of the first Bully EP, released alongside "Preacher Man" and "Beauty and the Beast." Produced by Ye with co-writing credits from his former manager and collaborator Hassan Khaffaf, the track is a concise, two-minute reflection on fame and consequence. The production of "Damn" is built around a melancholic piano melody and a raw, unpolished drum break. In its initial release on June 20, 2025, the song’s intro featured a prominent sample of "Feelings" by Nina Simone, specifically taken from her 1976 performance at the Montreux Jazz Festival. This sample choice was significant, marking the first time Ye had sampled Simone since 2016's "Famous." However, on November 13, 2025, the song was updated on all digital streaming platforms (DSPs) with the Nina Simone sample removed, likely due to clearance disputes. The updated version, which appears on the final Bully album, replaces the sample with a stripped-back instrumental section featuring additional keys played by Ye. Lyrically, the song features Ye grappling with his public image, delivering lines that have been described as "stream-of-consciousness" and "confessional." Read more on Last.fm.
